reasoning: Mamedyarov recently jumped 9 places to #11 in the March 2025 FIDE ratings after a 17-point gain at the Azerbaijan Championship, bringing his rating to 2748. He is currently in a 3-way tie at 2748 with Wesley So and Levon Aronian. As a 39-year-old player whose performance just saw a significant upward spike, regression to the mean dictates a high likelihood of dropping in rank relative to his peers. Furthermore, other super-GMs near his rating (like So, Aronian, and Dominguez) were active in the March 2025 American Cup, increasing the probability that one of them gained rating or would surpass him on FIDE's activity/age tie-breaks if he remains inactive. Thus, for the immediate April lists (April 6 and April 29), it is more likely than not that he falls below 11th. As the forecast horizon extends to late 2025 and 2026, the variance of outcomes increases; while long-term decline is possible, he also has more opportunities for another good tournament run to re-enter the top 11, pushing long-term probabilities steadily closer to 0.5 in accordance with increasing uncertainty.
